decisions.title: Use envctl database as durable migration source of truth
decisions.decision: Durable migration state is owned by envctl database tables and views; nu_plugin records render and command against that state.
decisions.rationale: The shared protocol manifest names envctl migration database as the source of truth and validates 14 record contracts for nu_plugin-facing shapes.
consequences: Migration commands must persist auditable state before downstream plugin display is considered complete.
Schema changes must preserve the shared protocol compatibility rule or move to a new major version.

decisions.title: Register migration artifacts with hashes, provenance, and validation links
decisions.decision: Generated artifacts are registered through the envctl artifact registry with package-relative paths, SHA-256 hashes, producer operations, provenance, graph edges, and validation rows.
decisions.rationale: REQ-024 passed with persisted paths, hashes, producers, contract ids, provenance, validation links, and fail-closed path rejection coverage.
consequences: Blocked paths such as secrets, private keys, .env files, and key material remain outside artifact registration.
Completion gates can compare registry hashes with on-disk artifacts instead of relying on file presence alone.

decisions.title: Keep target descriptors approval-gated and typed
decisions.decision: Migration targets are represented as registered descriptors with explicit target type, root path, safety mode, and maximum automatic risk.
decisions.rationale: The target registry passed with 5 registered descriptors covering 5 target types; the primary target is flexnetos-vs-lifeos at /home/flexnetos/FlexNetOS with safety mode approval-gated.
consequences: Artifact generation may read descriptor facts but should not infer target roots from ambient shell state.
Higher-risk migration operations must remain approval-gated according to descriptor policy.

decisions.title: Treat the contract manifest as the canonical artifact path map
decisions.decision: Task-local artifacts are generated under migration-artifacts/art-126_decision_log, while the contract-visible governance markdown is mirrored to migration-artifacts/09-governance/decision-log.md.
decisions.rationale: The execution packet requires art-126 task artifacts, and the contract manifest requires the governance decision log at migration-artifacts/09-governance/decision-log.md with ART-126_DECISION_LOG as producer.
consequences: Downstream task proofs can find task-local JSON and markdown evidence.
Contract completeness checks can find the required governance decision-log path.

decisions.title: Use proof records and heartbeat files as execution completion evidence
decisions.decision: A task is complete only when generated artifacts exist, registry registration reports matching hashes, validation evidence is linked, heartbeat state is updated, and proof_records contains the task proof.
decisions.rationale: The packet completion gate requires artifact existence, recorded hash, and linked validation evidence; the existing execution framework stores proofs and heartbeats per task.
consequences: Validation failures should be reflected in proof status instead of hidden behind generated files.
Rollback remains scoped to files added by this task and the proof ledger entry.

decisions.title: Keep filesystem artifact work inside allowed target and execution paths
decisions.decision: Decision-log generation writes only migration-artifacts, execution-framework logs, state, scripts, and proof records allowed by the task packet.
decisions.rationale: The packet scope permits migration-artifacts and execution-framework paths while blocking environment files, secrets, private keys, PEM files, and key files.
consequences: Secret-like paths are not scanned or registered as evidence.
The rollback plan can remove only ART-126-added files without touching unrelated package state.

decisions.title: Use reproducible SQLite smoke registration for artifact proof
decisions.decision: Artifact proof is generated by applying the package migrations to an in-memory SQLite database, inserting a task run and operation, and registering the generated files through the same ArtifactRegistry path used by REQ-024.
decisions.rationale: Current envctl database validation already proves the schema with 16 tables, 6 views, and seeded contract rows; using the same registry code gives deterministic proof without requiring a host database mutation.
consequences: This artifact task can be validated in isolation by VER-300 unit validation.
A later persistent envctl integration can replay the same artifact records using the recorded paths and hashes.
